title: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese

seo.title: How to Make Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese — a beloved Indian classic

description: Palak Paneer is a beloved Indian vegetarian dish featuring tender spinach puree and soft cubes of paneer cottage cheese. This creamy, vibrant curry is delicately spiced, offering a comforting and nutritious meal. It's a staple in Indian households, perfect for a wholesome family dinner.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spinach
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the cleaned spinach leaves and blanch for 2-3 minutes until wilted. Immediately drain the spinach and plunge it into a bowl of ice-cold water to retain its vibrant green color. Once cooled, drain well and blend into a smooth puree with a splash of water if needed. Set aside.

instructions.title: Fry the Paneer (Optional)
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee in a pan over medium heat. Add the cubed paneer and lightly fry until golden brown on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside. If you prefer softer paneer, skip frying and soak it in warm water for 15 minutes before adding to the curry later.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining oil or ghee. Once hot, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Then add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it turns translucent and light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and slit green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Tomato Masala
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil starts to separate from the mixture, which usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2 more minutes, ensuring the raw smell of the spices disappears.

instructions.title: Combine Spinach and Paneer
instructions.text: Add the spinach puree to the tomato masala. Mix thoroughly and cook for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. If the curry is too thick, add a little hot water to reach your desired consistency. Gently add the fried or soaked paneer cubes to the spinach curry. Stir carefully to coat the paneer.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the garam masala and heavy cream (if using). Simmer for another 2-3 minutes on low heat, allowing the paneer to absorb the flavors. Taste and adjust salt if necessary. Garnish with a swirl of cream or a sprinkle of fresh ginger juliennes before serving.

tips: For a vibrant green color, blanch spinach quickly and immediately plunge into ice water before pureeing. This shock helps set the color.
If fresh paneer is unavailable, use store-bought paneer. For extra softness, soak it in warm water for 15-20 minutes before adding to the curry.
Palak Paneer tastes even better the next day as the flavors deepen. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Serve hot with naan, roti, or rice.
article: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese is a beloved Indian Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
